sourceRef.citation: Kourovka Notebook, Problem 10.16 (Khukhro & Mazurov eds., ed.10)

statement.latex: A class of groups is called a direct variety\/ if it is closed under taking sub\-groups, factor-groups, and direct products (Yu.\,M.\,Gorchakov, Groups with Finite Classes of Conjugate Elements, Moscow, Nauka, 1978 (Russian)). It is obvious that the class of$FC$-groups is a direct variety. P.\,Hall (J. London Math. Soc., 34, no.\,3 (1959), 289--304) showed that the class of finite groups and the c...
